title: maughania helferiana
intro: Maughania helferiana, also known as Indian Kino or Maugania, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimalarial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of compounds such as quassin and maughanine. The health benefits of Maughania helferiana include its potential to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Maughania helferiana can be made into teas, infusions, and capsules, and are often used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
